articleSectionHeader.description: A digital signature is an encrypted seal that verifies identity, detects tampering, and holds legal validity under IT Act, eIDAS, and global standards.

mediaInfoCardData.description: Every digital signature is tied to a verified digital certificate issued by a certifying authority. This ensures that only authorized individuals can sign documents — eliminating impersonation risks and creating a secure trust framework between parties.
mediaInfoCardData.title: Authentic

mediaInfoCardData.description: Once a document is digitally signed, any attempt to modify it breaks the cryptographic seal and alerts all parties of potential tampering. This end-to-end integrity check protects sensitive data and ensures version control.
mediaInfoCardData.title: Tamper-Proof

mediaInfoCardData.description: Recognized by Global Compliance Standards Digital signatures comply with international frameworks like the IT Act (India), eIDAS (EU), and ESIGN/UETA (US). They carry the same legal standing as handwritten signatures and are admissible in court.
mediaInfoCardData.title: Legally Valid
